title: Checkpoint backlog overflow → SPAWN failure
symptom: All SPAWN requests return status=1 with empty body. Client receives 12-byte header indicating failure. void_term silently falls back to direct PTY. Today reproduced with 103 .bin files in /tmp/void_server_ckpt/.
root_cause: scan_and_restore_checkpoints() at src/void_server.c:1241 reads every /tmp/void_server_ckpt/*.bin on startup and calls alloc_session() for each. When ckpt count > VS_MAX_SESSIONS (64), the fixed-size g_sessions array fills entirely with tombstone 'restored' sessions (pty_fd=-1) and alloc_session() returns NULL for every subsequent SPAWN. Cleanup is only triggered on explicit VS_KILL (line 1043 unlink) — never on TTL, count limit, or shell-exit GC.
fix: prune_old_checkpoints(max_keep=32) called from main() before scan_and_restore_checkpoints. Tracked as VS-03.
fix_summary: prune_old_checkpoints(max_keep=32) added at void_server.c:684, called from main() before scan_and_restore_checkpoints. Verified: 40 dummies → 32 retained, 5/5 SPAWN status=0 baseline + post-prune against shipped /Applications binary.

title: Client socket fd leaked into spawned shell
symptom: After SPAWN, the spawned shell process (zsh child of forked handler) holds a file descriptor on /tmp/void_server.sock visible via lsof. Today observed: pid 68534 zsh has fd 6u pointing at sock device 0x6ec41ca0f820d2d7, inherited from handler pid 68528. Shell could read/write the unix socket directly, bypassing protocol.
root_cause: void_server.c accepts client fd at line 1261, forks per-client handler at line 1266 — handler inherits the accepted fd. Handler then calls spawn_session_pty() at line 806 → forkpty() at line 587 → execl() at line 613/615. The accepted client fd is NOT marked FD_CLOEXEC and is NOT explicitly closed before execl, so it survives into the post-exec shell process.
fix: After accept() at line 1261 set FD_CLOEXEC: fcntl(c, F_SETFD, fcntl(c, F_GETFD) | FD_CLOEXEC). Tracked as VS-02.
fix_summary: FD_CLOEXEC set on accepted client fd at void_server.c:1313 (after accept() in main loop). Verified: lsof on spawned zsh shows no unix socket fd inherited; void_term tab shells no longer hold /tmp/void_server.sock.

title: void_term silently falls back to direct PTY when server connect fails
symptom: void_term launches and renders correctly, acquires /tmp/void_term.lock, but lsof shows zero file descriptors on /tmp/void_server.sock even though void_server is running and accepting connections (verified by python test client SPAWN returning status=0). All spawned tabs become direct fork-pty children of void_term, not server-managed sessions, so they cannot survive a void_term crash. Today reproduced multiple times — Terminal.app heap-corruption crash killed void_term and ALL its child shells with it.
root_cause: sys_appkit.m void_server_ensure() at line 2553 calls void_server_try_connect() at line 2556 — on failure spawns daemon via void_server_spawn_daemon() at line 2528. Both paths return -1 silently with NO stderr output. The fallback at lines 3638-3649 catches void_server_spawn() failure and routes to tab_spawn_pty_profile() (direct PTY) without any diagnostic. Candidate root causes (unconfirmed): (a) timing race with daemon bind (100ms wait window too tight), (b) stale socket inode mismatch, (c) recvmsg blocking on SCM_RIGHTS handshake, (d) void_server_enabled() returning false at runtime due to env propagation. Diagnosis pending VS-01.
fix: FALSE POSITIVE — see VP-04. void_term DOES connect to /tmp/void_server.sock; the original observation was a debugging-method artifact in macOS lsof. Confirmed by VS-01 diagnosis 2026-04-12: void_term fd 4u shows unix socket connected via 0xself->0xpeer cross-referenced against listener fd in void_server. SPAWN handshake completes, PTY master arrives via SCM_RIGHTS at fd 8. Connect path is healthy. Real residual issue is observability (silent failures should log to stderr) — open as VS-19.
resolution: VS-01 runtime diagnosis confirmed void_term ↔ void_server connection works end-to-end. Original lsof-based detection was wrong (see VP-04).

title: macOS lsof hides path on client-side AF_UNIX sockets
symptom: `lsof -p <client_pid> | grep /tmp/void_server.sock` returns ZERO matches even when the client process is actively connected to /tmp/void_server.sock. Looks like 'client never connected'. False conclusion: 'connect bug exists, fix the client'.
root_cause: On macOS, lsof represents an accepted/connected AF_UNIX socket on the CLIENT side as `unix 0x<self_addr> -> 0x<peer_addr>` with an EMPTY NAME field. The pathname `/tmp/void_server.sock` appears only on (a) the listener fd in the server (bind+listen), and (b) accepted-child fds inside the per-client server fork. Client-side never shows the path. Today this caused 30+ minutes of false-positive debugging on the void_term ↔ void_server connect path (recorded as VP-03 false positive).
fix: To verify a client is connected, do: `lsof -p <client_pid> | grep unix` to find rows like `unix 0xAAA -> 0xBBB`, then `lsof /tmp/void_server.sock` to enumerate server-side accepted peers, and cross-reference 0xBBB against the server-side device IDs. If a match exists, the connection is real. Alternative: use `netstat -an | grep void_server` or read /proc-equivalent (which doesn't exist on macOS — use fs_usage with -f network).
prevention: Never conclude 'no fd on socket' from grepping for the path in client-side lsof on macOS.

title: void_server does not actually persist sessions across client crash → real crash resilience not achieved
symptom: When a client process is hard-killed (kill -9, e.g., Terminal.app crash takes void_term down), the spawned shell DIES within ~300ms. Empirical: SPAWN creates new zsh pid 73671 → SIGKILL client subprocess pid 73556 → 0.3s later pid 73671 not alive. The 'void_server is the answer to crash resilience' premise of rules.json VD6 is NOT met yet — server tracks sessions but they do not actually outlive the client.
root_cause: Two layered issues. (1) The PTY master is created via forkpty INSIDE the per-client forked handler (via spawn_session_pty at void_server.c:577-643 called from handle_spawn at line 798). The listener parent never gets a copy of the master fd because g_sessions diverges across the fork. (2) When the client socket dies abruptly, handle_client's read_exact returns <0, the loop at line 1058 breaks, the function's cleanup code (1084-1090) marks session attached_client_fd=-1 and checkpoints, then close(client_fd) and returns — and the handler PROCESS exits because there is no main loop in the child after handle_client returns. On exit, kernel closes the handler's s->pty_fd. The client side already lost its master fd at SIGKILL time, so master refcount = 0 → kernel sends SIGHUP to the slave session leader → shell dies. Comment at line 1084 says 'Mark any session this client owned as detached (but keep alive)' but the implementation does not actually keep it alive.
not_a_bug_case: In-process socket.close() with the SCM_RIGHTS-received PTY fd kept open does NOT kill the shell (verified — handler stays alive 4+ seconds, master fd remains functional, shell prints 'Restored session' prompt on read). This is because (a) the client process is still alive holding the master, refcount stays >= 1, AND (b) the server side handler does not detect graceful close-via-FIN as fast as it detects abrupt close-via-RST. The artificial in-process scenario is misleading; the kill-9 scenario is the canonical real-world test.
fix: open — architectural change needed in void_server.c. Three options: (A) eliminate per-client fork; run a single-process select() loop in the listener parent that owns ALL PTY masters in g_sessions and dispatches PTY-readable + client-readable events by fd. (B) keep fork-per-client but on client disconnect transfer the PTY master fd back to the listener via socketpair+SCM_RIGHTS so the listener keeps it alive, then dispatch ATTACH by handing the master back to a new client. (C) on client disconnect the handler does NOT exit; instead it enters a session-keeper loop polling only the PTY master, and re-accepts new clients (pre-fork accept-shared pattern). Selected: option A (AI-native, simplest data flow, single source of truth, best perf — no fork-per-client cost). Tracked as VS-17.
files: src/void_server.c:577-643 (spawn_session_pty forkpty — master only ends up in handler's fd table)
src/void_server.c:1058-1092 (handle_client — exits cleanly on disconnect, killing the master ref)
src/void_server.c:1260-1272 (per-client fork — root cause of the divergence)
src/void_server.c:1084-1090 (misleading 'keep alive' comment vs actual exit-then-die behavior)
verified: Crash sim 2026-04-12: spawn via subprocess child python → SIGKILL child → 73671 dead at +0.3s (still dead at +1.0s, +3.0s). Server LIST returns the session with proc_count=0 (tombstone). ATTACH against tombstone returns -2 (no live PTY) so void_term cannot resume — falls through to fresh spawn.
fix_summary: VS-17 — full void_server.c refactor to single-process select() loop. Removed fork-per-client, removed handle_client(), removed pump_sessions(). Added g_clients[VS_MAX_CLIENTS] with per-client recv buffer state, set_nonblock helper, client_try_dispatch / client_on_readable / client_on_disconnect / pump_one_session. Listener parent now owns every PTY master directly via forkpty (no intermediate fork). On client disconnect: free the slot, mark sessions detached, KEEP PTY masters open. Added FD_CLOEXEC on g_sock_listen, g_binwatch_fd, g_binwatch_kq, and forkpty master so single-process model doesn't leak fds into spawned shells. Periodic waitpid(-1, WNOHANG) reap loop replaces the old child-process-orphans-children trick. File grew 1335 → 1557 lines (+351 -72). Verified 2026-04-12: spawn via subprocess client → SIGKILL → shell pid 96100 ALIVE at +0.3/+1.0/+3.0/+5.0s; ATTACH from fresh client returns status=0 with valid PTY fd via SCM_RIGHTS. Shipped to /Applications/VOID.app/Contents/MacOS/void_server (running pid 61130, ppid=1).

title: void_server grid stride 불일치 — pump s->cols vs ATTACH VS_GRID_COLS
symptom: void_harness T1(debug) 테스트에서 첫 번째 명령(FIRST_CMD)은 성공하나 두 번째(SECOND_CMD)는 5초 폴링에도 grid에 나타나지 않음. grid dump에서 row 0만 정상, row 1+ 데이터 불일치.
root_cause: pump_one_session에서 grid write: idx = s->cur_row * s->cols + s->cur_col (stride=80). build_attach_body에서 grid read: s->grid[r * VS_GRID_COLS] (stride=400). row 0은 양쪽 offset 0으로 일치하나 row 1부터 write offset(80) ≠ read offset(400) → ATTACH가 빈 셀 반환.
fix: pump_one_session의 모든 grid 접근을 VS_GRID_COLS stride로 통일. scroll memmove, cell write, last-row clear 3곳 수정.
fix_summary: grid write: row*VS_GRID_COLS+col, scroll src: grid+VS_GRID_COLS, row_bytes: sizeof(VsCell)*VS_GRID_COLS. void_harness 6/6 ALL PASS.

title: hexa 컴파일러 0x hex literal 미파싱 → 생성 C에서 undeclared identifier
symptom: hexa build_c가 0x1100, 0xAC00 등 hex literal을 C 출력에서 x1100, xAC00으로 생성 → clang에서 'use of undeclared identifier' fatal error 20+건. 동시에 hexa parser도 hex literal 주변에서 'unexpected LB/RP' 28건 발생.
root_cause: hexa 컴파일러(~/Dev/hexa-lang/hexa)의 build_c 백엔드가 0x prefix를 C literal로 전달하지 못함. 0x를 제거하고 나머지를 identifier로 emit.
fix: void_main.hexa 내 모든 0x hex literal을 decimal로 수동 변환 (0x1100→4352, 0xAC00→44032, 0x10000→65536, 0x20000→131072 등). 변환 후 parse error 0건, clang clean build.
fix_summary: 28개 hex literal을 decimal로 변환. 향후 hexa 코드에서 0x notation 사용 금지.

title: CJK/emoji 텍스트 겹침 재발 — wide-char coverage 부족 의심
symptom: void_term 빌드 후 Claude Code TUI에서 배너 영역 텍스트 겹침 + 분홍색 블록 깨짐 발생. is_wide_cjk가 emoji 범위(U+1F300..U+1F9FF, U+2600..U+26FF 등)를 미커버.
root_cause: 조사 중. is_wide_cjk 함수가 CJK/Hangul/Hiragana/Katakana/Fullwidth만 커버하고 emoji/symbol 범위 누락. 또는 TUI가 ANSI cursor-movement CSI로 겹쳐쓰기를 하는 패턴에서 VT parser 부정확.
fix: open — emoji wide-char 범위 추가 또는 VT CSI 처리 정확성 검증 필요.

title: setNeedsDisplayInRect 부분 업데이트 → macOS 15+ layer-backed view에서 backing store 미보존으로 렌더 깨짐
symptom: 터미널 타이핑/스크롤 시 dirty 영역 이외의 셀이 깨지거나 사라짐. 부분적으로 빈 화면, 잔상 발생.
root_cause: hexa_appkit_term_flush()에서 dirty row 범위만 setNeedsDisplayInRect:로 마킹 → macOS가 non-dirty 영역의 backing store를 보존한다고 가정. macOS 15+ layer-backed view에서는 이 보장 없음 — AppKit이 dirty rect 외부를 클리어할 수 있어 렌더 내용 소실.
fix: flush에서 g_prev_grid_valid=0 + [v setNeedsDisplay:YES]로 매 프레임 전체 뷰 redraw. 델타 렌더링 캐시도 무효화하여 모든 셀 재페인트 보장.
fix_summary: setNeedsDisplayInRect: → setNeedsDisplay:YES + g_prev_grid_valid=0. 부분 업데이트 전면 폐기.
prevention: macOS NSView에서 setNeedsDisplayInRect:로 부분 업데이트 시 backing store 보존을 가정하지 말 것. 특히 layer-backed view (macOS 15+)에서는 전체 뷰 redraw가 유일하게 안전한 방법.

title: SCM_RIGHTS PTY fd 공유 시 서버·클라이언트 read() 경쟁 → 초기 프롬프트 소실
symptom: void_term 시작 시 셸의 최초 프롬프트(ghost@Mac ~ %)가 표시되지 않음. 첫 타이핑 후에야 프롬프트 출력.
root_cause: void_server가 SCM_RIGHTS로 PTY master fd를 클라이언트에 전달하지만, 서버도 같은 fd를 pump_one_session()에서 계속 read(). 동일 file description이므로 read()는 경쟁 — 서버가 먼저 호출하면 셸 초기 프롬프트 바이트를 소비하고 클라이언트는 빈 데이터를 받음.
fix: pump_one_session()에서 attached_client_fd >= 0이면 즉시 return. 클라이언트 연결 중에는 서버가 PTY를 읽지 않음. 클라이언트 detach 후 서버 grid 업데이트 재개.
fix_summary: pump_one_session 첫 줄에 `if (s->attached_client_fd >= 0) return;` 가드 추가.
prevention: SCM_RIGHTS로 fd를 전달한 후 원본 프로세스에서 같은 fd를 read()하지 말 것. dup된 fd는 독립이 아니라 동일 file description을 공유하므로 read() 경쟁이 발생한다.

title: applicationWillTerminate 우회 → 유령 세션 잔존
symptom: 앱 완전 종료 후 재시작하면 이전 세션 탭이 유령처럼 남아있음. 빈 셸 또는 이전 프로필 탭이 자동 복원.
root_cause: applicationWillTerminate:에서 세션 KILL을 시도하지만, auto-build hook의 hot-swap 경로가 _exit(0)으로 종료 → 핸들러 우회. force-quit(Cmd+Opt+Esc)도 마찬가지. 서버 세션은 살아남고 체크포인트 파일도 디스크에 잔존. 다음 fresh launch 시 auto-attach가 이 세션들을 탭으로 복원.
fix: 시작 시(is_shadow=false) auto-attach 전에 void_server_list→전체 KILL + /tmp/void_server_ckpt/*.bin 삭제. 종료 시 cleanup은 best-effort 유지하되, 시작 시 cleanup이 ground truth.
fix_summary: fresh launch 시 LIST→전체 KILL + ckpt 삭제 후 auto-attach (결과: 0 세션 → 새 탭 생성).
prevention: 데몬 세션 정리는 종료 시가 아닌 시작 시에 해야 안전. 종료 핸들러는 신뢰할 수 없음 (_exit, SIGKILL, force-quit 등).

title: standalone LF (\n without \r) → 테이블 점진적 밀림
symptom: 프로필 단축키로 cl 실행 시 테이블 행이 점진적으로 우측으로 밀림. 첫 3-4행은 정상, 이후 행은 이전 행 끝에서 시작.
root_cause:
void VT 파서가 LF(0x0A)를 cursor-down만 처리 (scr_cur_y++), CR(col 0 복귀) 안 함. onlcr ON이면 PTY가 
 변환하므로 문제없으나, raw PTY mode에서 cl이 standalone 
을 보내면 커서가 col 0으로 안 돌아감. PTY capture에서 offset 2303 이후 17개 standalone 
 확인 — 정확히 깨지기 시작하는 행과 일치.
fix: sys_pty.c hexa_pty_poll_read에서 standalone \n 앞에 \r 자동 삽입. right-to-left expand로 기존 데이터 보존.
fix_summary: read 후 standalone LF 앞에 CR 삽입. Terminal.app 동작 매칭.
prevention: 터미널 VT 파서에서 LF를 받을 때 CR을 자동 수행하거나, PTY read 후 LF→CRLF 정규화를 반드시 적용할 것.
